specFirst: This file was written BEFORE the code (PropostaAgentNewSolution3.md §2.5). The code is validated against it: each step declares inputs/outputs/gate, and an automated test checks that the implementation matches this spec. Maintenance changes the spec FIRST, then the code.
description: Step-based pipeline with consistent checkpoints. Each step reads ONLY the frozen files from the previous step and writes ONLY its own files (zero canonical state in task payloads). Every checkpoint has 3 faces: machine artifact (JSON/defs.ts with versioned schema), human summary (md), and deterministic gate (code, no LLM). Phase 1 implements up to the 2nd human checkpoint (E1+E2); E3..E7 are declared as planned.
principles: Compiler-style pipeline, not a task tree: no passive containers. Fan-out uses the collab-messages parallel system (REVISED 2026-07-08 - the proposal's 'list iteration inside the step' is not possible: LLM calls only happen via prompt_ready, one per step run; the parallel system adds bounded concurrency, slot cleanup and good UX). Disk artifacts + the finalize repair round keep the determinism the proposal wanted.
Fail early: repair means rerun step N with the gate error in context (1 retry); if it persists, show a visible checkpoint failure with a readable partial artifact. No LLM critic/repair.
Step is the maintenance unit: 1 folder per step (agent, prompt.md, schema, gate, test, fixture, readme, CHANGELOG). helpers/ is minimal and policy-frozen (it does not know step-specific logic).
System that builds systems: local examples or point fixes embedded in prompts/gates are forbidden; fixes generalize or they do not enter.
No console: everything goes to trace; errors appear objectively in the task.

UI: the step list is a tree ONLY for presentation (visual grouping derived from this flow.json, with no orchestration/dependency semantics). Step strings are translated to the user's language, as in v2.
CLI-like: commands such as /rebuild e2 rerun a step while skipping already completed ones; dirty propagation (inputsHash) prevents skipping steps when upstream changed.

conventions.module: {module} = module name proposed in E1 and normalized by the E1 gate (first disk write; no temporary folder).
conventions.pipelineState: l4/{module}/pipeline/pipeline.json = pipeline state: per step {status: pending|running|gate_failed|approved, inputsHash, artifactVersion, approvedAt, approvedBy: auto|human}. The runner is dumb: it reads pipeline.json and runs the next non-approved step. Resume means looking at the last approved gate.
conventions.artifacts: Phase artifacts in l4/{module}/pipeline/e{N}-*.{json,md}. Final artifacts (from E3 onward) use the SAME paths/formats as v2 (l4/{module}/ontology, l4/actors, l4/rules, l4/workflows, l4/operations, l4/{module}/journeys, l5/{module}) so Stage 2/3 do not change.
conventions.schemas: Versioned schemas/e{N}-*.schema.json files ($id with version). Schema and gate only change with a version bump + fixture update. Loosening a gate to pass a fix = upstream bug.
conventions.gates: 1 gate = 1 file = 1 .test.ts, inside the step folder. The gate validates schema + semantic invariants. Warnings do not block; errors block and trigger 1 retry with the error in context. IMPORTANT (2026-07-08): a gate-failed run with a retry in flight is completed-with-trace, NOT failed - update-status 'failed' marks the whole task as failed and orphans the retry (collab-messages updateStepStatus). Visible failure happens only when the retry also fails. This is safe because downstream never depends on run planIds, only on the eN-done anchors.
conventions.dirtyPropagation: Each step writes the hash of consumed inputs to pipeline.json. If an approved upstream artifact changes (for example, the E2 adjustment loop), downstream steps become dirty and runner/CLI refuses skip.
conventions.versioning: E2 adjustment-loop iterations generate e2-journeys.v{K}.json; 'approve' freezes the current version as e2-journeys.json and records the decision (source for the designContext in E3).
conventions.singleWritePath: Widgets NEVER write artifacts directly: every change (edit or adjustment prompt) passes through the adjustment agent + gate before being presented again.
conventions.rootPlan: agentNewSolution3 returns userLanguage, validPrompt/invalidReason, localized step titles, uiLabels for checkpoint widgets, and clarification 1 questions.
conventions.resume: An empty '@@newSolution3' (the runtime strips the mention) resumes the first module whose Phase 1 is incomplete instead of starting a new one, so the user does not repeat the clarification. beforePromptImplicit scans l4/{module}/pipeline/pipeline.json; a step counts as done when approved OR its lastGate.ok is true. Phase 1 resume re-enters at e2-journeys or the journeys checkpoint; phase-2 resume (2026-07-08) re-enters at the next unapproved/dirty phase-2 step (pipeline.json order) rebuilding the chain from that step on - the first rebuilt step runs immediately and the rest keep their eN-done anchor dependencies. A non-empty prompt always starts a new module.
conventions.doneAnchors: Every phase-2 step eN publishes a completed 'result' step with planId 'eN-done' when (and only when) its gate passes. Downstream steps dependsOn ['eN-done'], never on the agent step planId: retries and adjustment runs have dynamic planIds, and unlockWaitingDependencySteps only unlocks on a COMPLETED step per planId, so the anchor is the stable contract regardless of which run succeeded.
conventions.itemChains: REVISED 2026-07-08 (was: sequential chains). Fan-out (E3 entities, E5 workflows/operations) uses the collab-messages PARALLEL system (5 slots, slot reuse, finished children deleted): the plan/classification step hosts the fan-out under itself (compact selector args 'entity:X'/'workflow:X'/'operation:X'), so its completion waits for the fan-out and anchors the next step. Fan-out children NEVER return 'failed' (a failed child fails the parent/task) and never add steps; on gate failure they complete-with-trace. A no-LLM finalize step ('e3-finalize'/'e5-finalize', with 'e5-operations-phase' as the workflows-to-operations barrier) verifies every defs file on disk, runs ONE sequential repair round for missing items, then writes the summary .md, approves the pipeline step and emits the 'eN-done' anchor. Completed runs pass cleaner 'input_output' so LLM payloads leave the task record (DynamoDB 400KB).
conventions.designContextPlacement: DEVIATION from PropostaAgentNewSolution3 §3-E3 (designContext as an ontology item): agentChangeBackend treats EVERY file under l4/{module}/ontology/ as a data entity (cbShared.readBackendScan) and would fold a DesignContext pseudo-entity into aggregate derivation. The design context therefore lives where Stage 2/3 already read it: module.defs.ts designContext (assembled in E7 from e1-draft + e2 decisions + audit), plus the pipeline artifacts which remain on disk.
conventions.consumerContract: Verified against agentChangeFrontend/cfeCreateShared.ts and agentChangeBackend/cbShared.ts (2026-07-07): consumers require ontology entity files (entityId,title,kind,ownership,fields,statusEnum,lifecycleStates,eventPolicy), module.defs.ts (module block, designContext.userLanguage, ontology.entities index, relationships), workflows (workflowId,pageId,title,actors,operationIds,entities,rulesApplied,story,capabilities), operations (operationId,commandName,pageId,bffName,entity,kind,reads,writes,accessPattern{kind,keyField Entity.field,pagination,output},inputs{inputId,fieldRef,required,source},contextResolution,rulesApplied,story,capability), journeys defs (workspaces{workspaceId,title,actor,kind,entity,workflowId,operationIds,purpose}, navigationEdges advisory), and l5 todoFrontend/todoBackend ({layer,moduleName,owners{ownerType,ownerId,status:'toCreate'}} - the ONLY generation-status source; inline statusFrontend/statusBackend are legacy). bffName = {module}.{pageId}.{commandName}; pageId = owning workflowId (else operationId); commandName = operationId - all attached deterministically, never by the LLM. l4/actors, l4/rules and l5/process.defs.ts are contract/documentation artifacts (not machine-read by Stage 2/3).
conventions.i18n: No hard-coded language; step strings, human summaries, and checkpoint uiLabels are generated in the user's language (userLanguage captured in E1).

folderLayout.agentNewSolution3.ts: root: receives the prompt, registers the task, builds the visual step tree, delegates to the runner
folderLayout.skills/: platform.md (baseline: what NOT to model) + maintenance.md (mandatory LLM adjustment protocol)
folderLayout.helpers/: ns3Fs.ts (read/write/path hardening), ns3Ids.ts (id normalization), ns3Gate.ts (gate runner + retry), ns3Pipeline.ts (pipeline.json, inputsHash, dirty), ns3Trace.ts
folderLayout.schemas/: Versioned JSON Schemas for artifacts between steps
folderLayout.steps/e1-draft/: readme.md, agentNs3Draft.ts, prompt.md, gate.ts, gate.test.ts, fixture/
folderLayout.steps/e2-journeys/: readme.md, agentNs3Journeys.ts, prompt.md, gate.ts, gate.test.ts, fixture/, widgetNs3Journeys.ts/.less and widgetNs3JourneysLogic.ts
folderLayout.widgetNs3Journeys.ts/.less: human checkpoint 2 (graphical journeys + features/priorities + adjustment prompt)

treeOverview: phase-understanding (visual): e1-clarification -> e1-clarification-answer -> e1-draft (auto-approved, NO human checkpoint)
phase-business (visual): e2-journeys -> checkpoint-journeys (2nd and last human interaction; adjustment loop)

steps.goal: 1st human interaction. The agent container emits a runtime clarification payload, matching agentNewSolution2Requirements. Validate the prompt as early as possible: if invalid/insufficient (admission gate: empty, out of scope, no system intent), return an objective error + this clarification. Minimal opening questions; no architecture.
Task result created after the user submits clarification 1. This plan id is the dependency that unlocks e1-draft; e1-draft must not be manually moved to pending.

Additional human clarification opened only when the E1 gate finds a blocking question. The partial e1-draft.json/.md remains saved, pipeline.json keeps e1-draft as gate_failed, and the answer reruns E1 with blockingAnswers and previousModuleName.
Analyze the prompt and deliver the draft (understanding contract, 1 page): proposed module, problem, presumed actors, scope in/out, classified open questions, language.

checks: valid e1-draft schema
normalized and non-colliding module name
every open question is classified (blocking | assumed with default)
userLanguage is present
gate.onBlockingDoubt: opens an additional clarification instead of continuing

steps.goal: REMOVED (2026-07-06). Per PropostaAgentNewSolution3 §8 there are only TWO human interactions (clarification and journeys), so E1 is NOT a human checkpoint. E1 auto-approves on a green gate (approveNs3Step 'auto') and the pipeline goes straight to E2. The e1-draft.md remains on disk as the understanding contract / E2 input. widgetNs3Draft.* stays in the repo but is not wired.

Analyze/enrich user journeys and generate the feature catalog - WITHOUT ontology. Journeys by actor (steps with intent and result, no layout/page) + features with now/soon/later/never priority. Absorbs v2 discover-scope + recommend + decisions into ONE consistent delivery.

checks: valid e2-journeys schema
every feature is referenced by at least one journey step
every actor has at least one journey
priorities are complete (now/soon/later/never)
ids are normalized and unique
every e1 actor is present (removed actors require an explicit recorded decision)

steps.goal: 2nd human interaction - the most important business checkpoint. The agent emits a clarification payload owned by this step; beforeClarificationStep renders widgetNs3Journeys. The widget shows journeys by actor, feature priorities, editable notes/rules, and an adjustment prompt. Loop: user request -> a new dynamic e2-journeys adjustment step applies it -> gate -> new version v{K} presented again. Approval freezes the current version.

output.result: clarification UI submitted by the user; approval creates checkpoint-journeys-answer, adjustment creates a new e2-journeys run

steps.notes: singleWritePath: the widget never writes artifacts directly. All approvals/adjustments are stored as task steps and in e2-journeys-audit.json. Strong implementation pattern (2026-07-06): custom checkpoint UI is a clarification emitted in the owning agent interaction payload, as documented in skills/collab_messages.md and used by agentNewSolution2Requirements. Dynamic adjustment steps reuse agentNs3Journeys instead of a separate agent file, keeping generation/gate/write behavior in one maintenance unit.

steps.goal: Task result created only when the user approves the E2 journeys checkpoint. This is the dependency that unlocks E3; adjustment requests intentionally do not create this result.

savedArtifact: pipeline.json with e2-journeys and checkpoint-journeys approvedBy=human

steps.goal: Ontology plan + per-entity generation. The plan call reads e2-journeys.json (+ e1-draft.json) and produces e3-model.json: the module block (title/purpose/businessDomain/languages/visualStyle), a slim entity index (entityId PascalCase, title, description, kind core|supporting|event|metric|mdm, ownership, statusEnum/lifecycleStates, sourceRefs to journeys/features/businessRules) and relationships. Then a sequential item chain generates one FULL entity per file (fields with types uuid|string|text|number|money|boolean|date|datetime|<EntityId>, enums, audit fields, statusEnum aligned with journey business rules).

output.result: entity model plan, then one canonical entity per file

checks: valid e3-model schema
PascalCase unique entity ids; anti Uc*/usecase/verb guard
relationships resolve to declared entities
every non-never E2 feature is covered by >=1 entity sourceRef (warning)

per entity: valid e3-entity schema, unique camelCase fieldIds, valid field types (<EntityId> refs resolve), enum fields carry enum values, statusEnum consistent with lifecycleStates, id + createdAt/updatedAt audit fields present

steps.goal: Single call: actor roster (roleScope '{module}:{actorId}'), the consolidated rule set (E2 per-journey businessRules are the primary source - every journey rule maps to a ruleId or is explicitly absorbed) and external references (MDM/horizontals/plugins/agents) that E7 writes into module.defs.ts approvedArtifacts.

checks: valid e4 schema
every E2 actor is in the roster (extra supporting actors allowed)
roleScope format {module}:{actorId}
unique camelCase ruleIds
every rule appliesTo resolves to an E3 entity
every E2 journey businessRule maps to a ruleId (unmapped = warning)

steps.goal: Classification call first: e5-classification.json maps every non-never E2 feature to workflows (stateful, with owned operationIds) and standalone operations, declaring actor/entity/kind per item. Then a sequential item chain: one LLM call per workflow (states/transitions aligned with the primary entity lifecycle, embedded story) and per operation (reads/writes, accessPattern with keyField 'Entity.field', inputs with declared sources, contextResolution, acceptanceAssertions derived from journey step results). pageId/commandName/bffName/capability are attached deterministically after each call - never by the LLM.

output.result: classification, then one behavior per file

checks: valid schemas (classification, workflow, operation)
every non-never E2 feature realized by >=1 workflow/operation
every workflow has non-empty operationIds and every referenced operation is classified
workflow states align with the primary entity statusEnum/lifecycleStates
transitions reference declared states and classified operations
operation kind in create|update|delete|query|view
reads/writes/entity resolve to E3 entities
accessPattern.keyField is 'Entity.field' with a real field
inputs sources valid; required inputs have inputId+fieldRef+source
bffName == {module}.{pageId}.{commandName} (deterministic attach re-checked)
every entity with standalone write operations has a managedEntities entry (deletionPolicy delete|inactivate|immutable)
policy delete requires a delete operation; inactivate requires a statusEnum inactivationState + update operation; immutable requires a reason and forbids delete
self-transitions (from == to) are a workflow gate error
finalize demotes workflow-listed operations without a real transition (create trigger exempt) and re-validates managedEntities post-demotion
managedEntities requirement scoped by E3 entity kind (mdm = error; core with statusEnum = warning)

steps.goal: Single call over frozen workflows/operations + E2 journeys: consolidated navigation map - workspaces (page grouping unit read by agentChangeFrontend: workspaceId/title/actor/kind/entity/workflowId/operationIds/purpose), landings per actor and navigation edges (advisory for Stage 2).

checks: valid e6 schema
every operation appears in >=1 workspace
workspace workflowId/operationIds/actor/entity resolve
unique workspaceIds
every actor with now-priority behaviors has a landing (warning)
edges reference existing pageIds (warning)
workspace kind derived deterministically from the classification (workflow-owned ops -> workflow; standalone create+update on the entity -> entityManagement; else operation)
entityManagement workspaces never declare workflowId

steps.goal: Deterministic (no LLM) global recheck + closing. Reads ONLY saved files: cross-consistency checks (v2 code style: workflow.operation.unknown, entity.ref.unknown, capability coverage, journey workspace coverage, bff naming) written to behavior-health-report.json (errors block). Then assembles module.defs.ts (module block from e3-model, designContext from e1-draft + e2 decisions/audit, ontology index from entity files, relationships, approvedArtifacts from e4 refs), seeds l5 todoFrontend/todoBackend (one owner per workflow/operation, status toCreate) and l5 process.defs.ts, and writes the reopenable e7-validation.md summary.
input: all saved l4 artifacts (files only, never payload)

checks: health report has zero errors (warnings do not block)
module.defs.ts index matches the ontology files on disk
todo owners exactly match generated workflows+operations

commands: /rebuild e{N} (reruns the step; refuses if upstream is dirty)
/replay e{N} fixture={name} (golden run: runs the step on the fixture and performs a structural invariant diff)

handoffArtifacts.finalProduces: identical to agentNewSolution2 (Stage 2/3 do not change)
